name: Minced Squab with Lettuce Cups
recipeIngredient: 10 Fresh water chestnuts, peeled and minced
6 Chinese black mushrooms, soaked, squeezed dry, and minced
6 Dried oysters (optional), soaked, squeezed dry, and minced
½ teaspoon Fresh ginger root, minced
½ cup Chicken broth reduced from squab bones
2 tablespoons Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
1 teaspoon Cornstarch dissolved in 1 tablespoon cold chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
This dish is frequently served at special occasions in Chinese restaurants. It serves 6.
Using a thin cleaver, cut away the wings and thigh joints and pull off the skin, cutting where necessary. Set the skin aside. Bone the breasts. Scrape all the meat you can from the bone. To remove the leg meat, make a slit lengthwise to the bone and pull the meat away, cutting it off as needed. Remove any tendons from each leg. Bone the second squab and add bones, feet, heads, and wings to 1 cup chicken broth. Reduce the liquid to ½ cup.
Finely mince the squab meat. Set aside. Thinly slice the skin. Stir-fry the skin in a little oil until fat renders and skin crisps, about 10 minutes. As the skin browns, move it up the side of the wok. Set the crisped skin aside. The preceding steps can be done up to 8 hours in advance. Keep the minced squab refrigerated if it must stand longer than 1 hour.
Stir-fry the squab in oil with garlic over high heat until lightly browned. Add remaining minced ingredients, mix well and add reduced chicken broth, sugar, soy sauce, rice wine or sherry, and oyster sauce. Bring to a boil and stir in cornstarch mixture. Stir in crisped skin and transfer the mixture to a serving plate. Serve with lettuce cups. Each diner wraps some of the squab mixture in a lettuce cup.
